text: The following events were reported: Marine Thunderstorm Wind, Waterspout. These occurred near station USW00012844, approximately 19.729 km away, between 2021-04-11 14:02:00 and 2021-04-11 14:55:00.Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: A shortwave trough moving over the eastern Gulf of Mexico developed a mesoscale convective complex that moved through South Florida. Environmental parameters were favorable for the development of strong to severe storms, which produced a waterspout and gusty winds as they moved offshore into the Atlantic waters. A shortwave trough moving over the eastern Gulf of Mexico developed a mesoscale convective complex that moved through South Florida. Event Narrative: A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 49 mph (43 knots) was measured by CWOP Mesonet station EW1119 in Palm Beach at 3:33 PM EDT. Broadcast meteorologist reported brief waterspout just offshore Highland Beach associated with strong to severe thunderstorms along the coastal Atlantic waters. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 56 mph (49 kt) was measured by the WxFlow mesonet station XOAK, Oakes Substation at 3:02 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 63 mph (55 kt) was measured by the Weatherflow Mesonet station XJUP, Juno Beach Pier at 3:05 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 67 mph (58 kt) was measured by the NOAA/NCDC C-MAN station LKWF1 at Lake Worth Pier at 3:36 PM EDT.

Broadcast meteorologist reported brief waterspout just offshore Highland Beach associated with strong to severe thunderstorms along the coastal Atlantic waters. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 56 mph (49 kt) was measured by the WxFlow mesonet station XOAK, Oakes Substation at 3:02 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 63 mph (55 kt) was measured by the Weatherflow Mesonet station XJUP, Juno Beach Pier at 3:05 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 67 mph (58 kt) was measured by the NOAA/NCDC C-MAN station LKWF1 at Lake Worth Pier at 3:36 PM EDT.

A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 67 mph (58 kt) was measured by the NOAA/NCDC C-MAN station LKWF1 at Lake Worth Pier at 3:36 PM EDT.

Event Narrative: A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 49 mph (43 knots) was measured by CWOP Mesonet station EW1119 in Palm Beach at 3:33 PM EDT. Broadcast meteorologist reported brief waterspout just offshore Highland Beach associated with strong to severe thunderstorms along the coastal Atlantic waters. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 56 mph (49 kt) was measured by the WxFlow mesonet station XOAK, Oakes Substation at 3:02 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 63 mph (55 kt) was measured by the Weatherflow Mesonet station XJUP, Juno Beach Pier at 3:05 PM EDT. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 67 mph (58 kt) was measured by the NOAA/NCDC C-MAN station LKWF1 at Lake Worth Pier at 3:36 PM EDT.
